title: silene microsperma subsp. maritima
intro: Silene microsperma subsp. maritima, also known as sea catchfly or sea campion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Silene microsperma subsp. maritima is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
